page: Google Cloud Platform c4-highcpu-16 is a compute-optimized virtual machine instance belonging to the c4 server family. It is powered by an Intel Xeon 8581C x86_64 processor operating at 2.3 GHz. The hardware configuration features 16 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 8 physical cores with 2 threads per core. Memory capacity is 32.0 GB, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per core. The processor includes a 260 MB L3 cache, 2 MB L2 cache, and 48 KB L1d cache. It supports advanced instruction sets including Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512), Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX), and cryptographic acceleration via VAES and SHA_NI. This instance does not include local storage or integrated GPUs.

Benchmark evaluations indicate strong single-threaded capabilities. The instance achieves a Geekbench single-core score of 2314.0 and a single-threaded bzip3 decompression rate of 14.9 GB/sec, both placing in the top 10% tier. Single-threaded gzip compression is also strong at 34.81 GB/sec, placing in the top 25%. Multi-core performance is moderate, with a Geekbench multi-core score of 13758.0 and a stress-ng multi-core CPU score of 17354.28, both falling in the middle 50% average. Memory bandwidth tests show 1361.92 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 123.31 GB/sec for uncached large blocks. For small-scale language model workloads, the instance demonstrates a text generation speed of 336.56 tokens/sec for a 135M parameter model, which is in the top 10% tier, though prompt processing for a medium 7B model averages 101.48 tokens/sec.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the c4-highcpu-16 offers a high compute-to-memory ratio, making it resource-efficient for CPU-bound tasks that do not require large memory footprints. However, the lack of local NVMe storage and bundled public IPv4 addresses represents a tradeoff, requiring external storage attachments and network configurations for full deployment. The instance is suited for workloads such as text processing, single-threaded data decompression, software compilation (Clang benchmark score of 20129.0), and small-scale LLM inference. It also supports standard web serving and database operations, as reflected by its average Redis SET throughput of 24.68 million operations per second and static web serving throughput of 32.91 GB/sec.
description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highcpu-16 is an x86_64 compute-optimized server powered by an Intel Xeon 8581C processor running at 2.3 GHz. It features 16 dedicated vCPUs across 8 physical cores, 32.0 GB of RAM, and a 260 MB L3 cache, with no local storage or GPUs. The instance exhibits strong single-core performance, achieving top-tier results in Geekbench single-core (2314.0) and single-threaded bzip3 decompression (14.9 GB/sec). Multi-core performance and memory bandwidth align with mid-range averages, including a Geekbench multi-core score of 13758.0. The server is qualitatively cost-efficient for compute-heavy tasks with low memory requirements, though the lack of local storage is a notable tradeoff. It is suited for software compilation, text processing, small-scale language model inference, and web serving.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highcpu-16 is an x86_64 compute-optimized server with 16 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and strong single-core performance, suited for compilation and text processing.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highcpu-16 is an x86_64 compute-optimized server with 16 vCPUs and 32 GB RAM, featuring strong single-core performance.
tagline: A compute-optimized x86_64 server featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors, high single-core performance, and a balanced 2 GB RAM per core ratio.
bullet_points: 16 dedicated vCPUs and 32 GB RAM powered by an Intel Xeon 8581C processor.
Advanced instruction support including AVX-512 and Intel AMX.
Top-tier single-core performance in Geekbench and bzip3 decompression.
No local storage or integrated GPUs, requiring external storage attachments.
Suited for software compilation, text processing, and small-scale LLM inference.
